terms.definition: Linear regression with an L2 penalty λ||β||₂² added to the squared-error loss; also called Tikhonov regularization or weight decay in linear models.
Linear regression with an L1 penalty λ||β||₁ added to the squared-error loss; tends to produce sparse coefficient vectors.
Penalty proportional to the sum of squared coefficients; the canonical Ridge penalty on linear model weights.
Penalty proportional to the sum of absolute coefficient values; the canonical Lasso penalty that can zero out features.
Scalar λ (alpha in scikit-learn) controlling penalty strength; λ = 0 is unregularized OLS, larger λ shrinks coefficients more.
